title: Init uses packet-only bootstrap; refresh builds full graphs
summary: kage init should prioritize fast first use: install policy/settings, migrate or generate repo memory packets, write lightweight packet indexes, and use packet only recall for the preview. It must not build the full knowl
body:
kage init should prioritize fast first use: install policy/settings, migrate or generate repo memory packets, write lightweight packet indexes, and use packet-only recall for the preview. It must not build the full knowledge graph or code graph during init. kage index, kage refresh, doctor, and normal recall remain the full graph-building paths. Tradeoff: after init, validation/doctor may report graph/code-graph indexes missing until the user runs refresh or index.
Evidence: Implemented by adding packet-only index building and calling indexProject(project, { graphs: false }) from initProject in mcp/kernel.ts; covered by mcp/kernel.test.ts asserting init does not create graph.json or code_graph/graph.json.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp
